methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $96M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Antares is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Jordan Bramble and Julia DeWahl, having raised $96M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 11 companies across nuclear energy—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private, including Type One Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power—though direct same-stage comparables in this sector remain sparse.

The primary applicable method is stage-progression valuation, which applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $96M raised baseline, yielding $182M. A peer-median approach was not viable: while 8 private nuclear peers exist, filtering by stage and valuation tier produced insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3) to establish a reliable median, making sector-wide aggregation inappropriately broad and potentially misleading.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $155M to $210M, centered at $182M. This range rests on a single method, reflecting methodological constraints rather than consensus. Key uncertainties include the strength of Series B step-up dynamics within frontier nuclear specifically—a subset with limited transaction history—and the absence of tightly-matched peer comparables. Further valuation confidence would require either additional transaction data within nuclear energy or broader multi-sector frontier-tech triangulation.
